system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

vertices

    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6 (same as 2)

    // Block 1 - bend roof connecting block 0 to bend

    (  0  25 -25)   // 9 (same as 5/1)
    ( 25  25 -25)   // 10
    ( 25  25  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - bend transition (goes down in -y)

    (  0 -25 -25)   // 13 (same as 6/2)
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - outlet straight duct extending in -y
    (  0 -125  25)  // 16
    (  0 -125 -25)  // 17
    ( 25 -125 -25)  // 18
    ( 25 -125  25)  // 19
    (-500 -50  25)  // 20
    (-500 -50 -25)  // 21
    (-500 -125 -25) // 22
    (-500 -125  25)  // 23
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct (along +x)
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend roof region
    hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend transition
    hex (19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet duct
    hex (23 22 21 20 19 18 17 16) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (20 21 22 23)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces

            (0 1 5 4)   // front wall (+z)
            (0 3 2 1)   // back wall (-z)
            (1 2 6 5)   // left wall (-y)
            (4 5 6 7)   // right wall (+y)

            (5 6 10 9)  // right wall (+y)

            (8 11 9 10) // back wall (-z)

            (14 15 19 18) // right wall (+y)

);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (23 22 21 20 19 18 17 16) ... has inward-pointing faces`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Error 1 Deep Dive

The error is:
```
Block hex (23 22 21 20 19 18 17 16) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(23 20 19 22) 4(22 21 18 19) 4(20 23 16 22) 4(21 20 17 21)
```

The block definition `hex (23 22 21 20 19 18 17 16)` has inward-pointing normals. Let me trace the vertices:

- 23: (-500, -125, 25)
- 22: (-500, -125, -25)
- 21: (-500, -75, -25)
- 20: (-500, -75, 25)
- 19: (25, -125, 25)
- 18: (25, -125, -25)
- 17: (25, -75, -25)
- 16: (25, -75, 25)

Bottom face: `(23 22 21 20)` — all at x=-500, winding from y=-125 to y=-75. Normal via right-hand rule: (22-23)×(21-23) = (0,50,0)×(0,50,-25) = (-1250, -1250, 0) → points in -x direction. The block extends in +x, so -x IS the outward normal. ✓

Top face: `(19 18 17 16)` — all at y=-125, winding from x=25 to x=-500? Wait, 19=(25,-125,25), 18=(25,-125,-25), 17=(25,-75,-25), 16=(25,-75,25). All at x=25! This is a **degenerate face** — all four vertices share x=25, meaning the top face collapses to a line/curve, not a quadrilateral.

The fundamental problem revealed over all 10 attempts is that **Block 3 is geometrically invalid**. Vertices 16-19 all have x ∈ {0,25}, and vertices 20-23 all have x=-500. There is no valid hexahedral block that can bridge this geometry — the "L-shape" post-bend region cannot be represented as a single convex hexahedron.

### Recommended Solution

The root cause is that the block topology is fundamentally wrong. After 9 failed attempts at vertex reordering, the geometry itself doesn't support a valid single-hexahedron representation for the post-bend duct.

**Restructure the block topology to use only 2 blocks:**

- **Block 0**: Inlet straight duct along +x: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — the straight duct from x=-50 to x=0
- **Block 1**: Combined bend + outlet duct: This replaces the old Blocks 2 and 3. It spans from the bend corner (x=0, y=-25) down to y=-125 and also extends in -x to -500. Define it as a large block covering approximately x=-500..25, y=-125..25. However, this won't work either since vertices 16-19 are at x=0/25 and vertices 20-23 are at x=-500.

**The correct approach:** Adopt the **exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial verbatim**. The tutorial already contains a working 4-block decomposition for this exact L-bend geometry. Copy the tutorial's `blocks` and `boundary` sections exactly, keeping only the vertex coordinates from your current file. The tutorial's block definitions are proven to work — they avoid degenerate faces and have correct winding.

If copying the tutorial isn't possible, the minimal fix is to **remove Block 3 entirely** and merge everything into 2 blocks:

- **Block 0**: `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` — inlet duct
- **Block 1**: Everything else as a single large block `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19 11 10 9 8 7 6 13 12)` — but this is complex.

**Best recommendation:** Since the user said "Follow the same geometry as the tutorial," obtain the official squareBend tutorial's `blockMeshDict` and use it verbatim. The current custom block definition has been attempted 10 times with persistent failures — the geometry/topology needs to match the proven tutorial exactly.

## Error 2

This is a cascading error from blockMesh failing. It will resolve automatically once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary

1. **Replace the entire `blocks` section** in `blockMeshDict` with the proven squareBend tutorial block definitions. The tutorial's 4-block topology is the only reliable way forward given that 9 attempts at manual fixes have all failed due to geometric constraints.

2. **Keep all vertex coordinates (0-23)** as they are.

3. **Re-run `blockMesh`** before attempting `rhoSimpleFoam`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
